2604.07269.title: Joint Optimization of Reasoning and Dual-Memory for Self-Learning Diagnostic Agent
2604.07269.paper_summary: Clinical expertise improves not only by acquiring medical knowledge, but by accumulating experience that yields reusable diagnostic patterns. Recent LLMs-based diagnostic agents have shown promising progress in clinical reasoning for decision support. However, most approaches treat cases independently, limiting experience reuse and continual adaptation. We propose SEA, a self-learning diagnostic agent with cognitively inspired dual-memory module. We design a reinforcement training framework tailored to our designed agent for joint optimization of reasoning and memory management. We evaluate SEA in two complementary settings. On standard evaluation with MedCaseReasoning dataset, SEA achieves 92.46% accuracy, outperforming the strongest baseline by +19.6%, demonstrating the benefit of jointly optimizing reasoning and memory. On the long-horizon with ER-Reason dataset, SEA attains the best final accuracy (0.7214) and the largest improvement (+0.35 Acc@100), while baseline methods show limited or unstable gains. Expert evaluation further indicates that rules consolidated from SEA show strong clinical correctness, usefulness and trust, suggesting that the induced rules in dual-memory module are reliable and practically meaningful. Overall, SEA improves both diagnostic reasoning ability and continual learning by effectively transforming experience into reusable knowledge.
2604.07269.paper_summary_zh: 臨床專業知識的提升不僅來自於醫學知識的獲取，還來自於積累可重用的診斷模式的經驗。最近基於大型語言模型的診斷代理在臨床推理和決策支持方面顯示出令人鼓舞的進展。然而，大多數方法將案例獨立處理，限制了經驗的重用和持續適應。我們提出了SEA，一個具有認知啟發的雙記憶模組的自學習診斷代理。我們設計了一個針對我們設計的代理的強化訓練框架，以便共同優化推理和記憶管理。我們在兩個互補的環境中評估SEA。在使用MedCaseReasoning數據集的標準評估中，SEA達到了92.46%的準確率，超越了最強基準19.6%的表現，展示了共同優化推理和記憶的好處。在使用ER-Reason數據集的長期評估中，SEA達到了最佳的最終準確率（0.7214）和最大的改進（+0.35 Acc@100），而基準方法則顯示出有限或不穩定的增益。專家評估進一步表明，從SEA整合的規則顯示出強大的臨床正確性、有用性和可信度，這表明雙記憶模組中誘導的規則是可靠且具有實際意義的。總體而言，SEA通過有效地將經驗轉化為可重用的知識，提升了診斷推理能力和持續學習能力。
2604.07269.author: Bingxuan Li et.al.
2604.07269.authors: Bingxuan Li, Simo Du, Yue Guo
